steps.title: Identify the conventional wisdom and Behrenfeld's challenge
steps.content: Text 1’s conventional wisdom holds that one phytoplankton species should outcompete and displace the rest. Text 2 shows Behrenfeld’s team directly contradicting this: because phytoplankton are microscopic, they are spaced far apart in dense water, making movement and interaction difficult — so direct competition “happens much less than previously thought.”

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Check how each choice describes Behrenfeld’s response to the conventional wisdom:
  • Choice A — ‘By arguing that it is based on a misconception about phytoplankton species competing with one another’: Accurately captures Behrenfeld’s point — the conventional view overestimates how often phytoplankton actually compete, which is the core misconception Text 2 identifies. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘By asserting that it fails to recognize that routine replenishment of ocean nutrients prevents competition between phytoplankton species’: Text 2 never mentions nutrient replenishment; this mechanism is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘By suggesting that their own findings help clarify how phytoplankton species are able to compete with larger organisms’: Both texts discuss competition among phytoplankton species only; neither mentions competition with larger organisms. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘By recommending that more ecologists focus their research on how competition among phytoplankton species is increased with water density’: Reverses Text 2’s argument — Behrenfeld argues water density decreases, not increases, competition. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
